Starbucks College Achievement Plan

Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.

Activity Basics

Question

Activity Description (Short)

Starbucks College Achievement Plan (SCAP) A first-of-its-kind education program, SCAP continues to demonstrate significant impact through enrollment growth, degree completion, and expanded access to higher education for Starbucks partners. In May 2026, the program celebrated its largest graduatin...

Question

Activity Start Date / End Date

Start Date

2025-07-01 (inferred from FY26 survey window)

End Date

2026-06-30 (inferred from FY26 survey window)

Question

Public Service or Community Engagement

Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.

Question

Programs/Initiatives

Starbucks Initiatives

Question

Faculty/Staff

  • Laura Wapelhorst (Assistant Vice President) - laura.wapelhorst@asu.edu
  • Additional ASU collaborators from survey: Strong partnerships across ASU’s Academic Enterprise, Learning Enterprise, and Knowledge Enterprise advance the Starbucks relationship. This includes, but is not limited to: EdPlus, University Affairs, Media Relations and Strategic Communication, ASU Brand, ASU Foundation, College of Global Futures, School of Sustainability, W. P. Carey School of Business, The College of Integrative Arts and Sciences, and Fulton Schools of Engineering. We regularly engage additional faculty, staff, and university partners to assist with emerging opportunities that support strategic priorities for our partnership with Starbucks.
  • ASU Institutional Relationship Manager: Laura Wapelhorst
